Drop a CSV or Excel file here, or browse to upload.
Bring in existing survey data and run the same quality checks as native ReliCheck surveys.
Upload a CSV or Excel file of existing survey data, mark which columns are Likert items, and run the same reliability and validity analysis you get from native ReliCheck surveys.

Upload an existing survey

Bring a survey you built in SurveyMonkey, Qualtrics, Google Forms, Microsoft Forms, or anywhere else into ReliCheck. Paste the question text or upload a Qualtrics .qsf file, and we'll extract the questions and scales for you.

We send the text to AI for question extraction. No response data is sent. Maximum about 120,000 characters.
Drop a Qualtrics .qsf file here
Or click to choose a file. ReliCheck reads the JSON, extracts the question text, and brings it into your survey.
Direct connectors for SurveyMonkey, Qualtrics, Google Forms, Microsoft Forms, and Typeform are on the roadmap. For now, use the Paste survey text tab and copy the question text from the preview page of your survey, or export a Qualtrics .qsf and use the Upload .qsf file tab.

Create a new survey

Default Likert scale
Applies to every Likert question unless that question overrides it. Has no effect on single-choice, multi-select, or open-ended items.
Anonymous mode (k-anonymity)
Suppresses any rollup bucket whose count is below the threshold below. Useful for HR engagement and IRB-sensitive research where small groups risk re-identification. Buckets with zero respondents stay as zero. Set to 0 to disable.
Common HR defaults are k = 5 or k = 10. Research IRBs often require k = 5 minimum.
Key Drivers digest email
Auto-send a one-time digest email to the survey owner when the response count first crosses the threshold below. The email previews the top three drivers by simple correlation and links to the full Key Drivers tab. Set to 0 (Disabled) to turn off.
Fires once per threshold. To re-arm the digest after it has sent, raise the threshold to the next bucket. The Key Drivers tab needs at least 15 responses to compute a stable ranking, so 25 is the recommended floor.
Random assignment & quotas
Randomly assign each respondent to one of the experimental arms below. Set a quota per arm to cap recruitment per cell. Once a cell fills, new respondents are routed to the remaining arms. The assigned arm is recorded on each response and is available as a grouping field in analytics.
Manager dashboard & action templates
When this survey runs, the dashboard surfaces the composite Likert score, places it in a band (Low / Borderline / Healthy / Strong), and recommends 3 to 5 next-step actions tailored to the construct below. Useful for handing managers a one-glance read with a clear next move.
Picks which action library to draw recommendations from. Doesn't change how reliability is computed.
Public take URL
The link respondents use to take this survey. Distribution channels and tracked invitations live on the Distribute view; this is just the raw link plus a copy button.
https://relichecksurvey.com/s/...
Read-only dashboard sharing
Share a password-protected, expiring read-only copy of the analytics dashboard with stakeholders who do not have a ReliCheck account. Configure from the Analyze tab Share card.
Changes save automatically.

Survey purpose
No purpose set. Add a short statement of what decision this survey is meant to inform.
Construct mapping

A construct is the underlying concept an item is meant to measure (for example, Belonging, Engagement, Self-Efficacy). Tagging each item with its construct lets ReliCheck compute factor-level reliability and run confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) on the Validity tab.

Use Suggest constructs to have AI group your items in one step, or set the Construct field by hand on any item below. Items that share a construct name are treated as members of the same factor.

Survey Distribution

Choose how to share this survey. Each method is its own dashboard with its own setup, send, and tracking.

Contacts
0
Invitations sent
0
Completed
0
Reminder schedule
Off

Cover page (optional)

Show an introduction screen before respondents see the questions. Useful for IRB informed-consent text, sponsor disclosures, or a friendly welcome. The page appears on every distribution channel below (web link, email, QR, Slack, embed, AI chat take).

Body
Up to 5,000 characters. Plain text only; HTML is escaped.
Required forces the respondent to check the box before Continue is clickable. Optional shows the checkbox but the respondent can continue without checking it. None hides the checkbox entirely and shows just a Continue button.

Create Distribution

Choose how you want to send your survey and collect responses.

Web Link

Anyone with this link can take the survey. Copy it, embed it, or share it however you want.

QR Code

Print on a poster, flyer, or receipt. Mobile cameras open the survey instantly.

Website Embed

Drop this iframe snippet into a website, course page, or intranet. The survey renders in line on the page.

Calendar Event

Attach the survey to a meeting invite. The downloaded .ics file imports into Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar, and any other calendar app. Useful for interviews, 360s, focus groups, and post-meeting evaluations.

Social Media

Open a share window for LinkedIn, X, or Facebook with the survey link and your message pre-filled.

API and Webhooks

Receive response events (created, completed) at any HTTPS endpoint. Useful for syncing to your HRIS, CRM, or Zapier.

Contacts

Add people one at a time, paste a list, or import contacts to prepare your distribution.

Paste a list

One contact per line. Use email, or email,name, or email,name,external_ref. Lines starting with # are skipped.

Invitations

Send invitations to active contacts and monitor delivery results in one place.

Reminders

Schedule friendly follow-ups for contacts who have not responded yet.

Automatic reminders help improve response rates without manual follow-up.

Slack and Teams channels

Paste a Slack incoming webhook or a Microsoft Teams webhook URL to push the survey-take card directly into a channel. Each pulse wave fires here too.

Add a Slack or Teams channel

In Slack: Apps -> Incoming Webhooks, pick a channel, copy the webhook URL. In Teams: channel menu -> Connectors -> Incoming Webhook, copy the URL.

We accept Slack (hooks.slack.com) and Teams (*.webhook.office.com) URLs. The destination type is detected automatically.

Pulse schedules

Schedule the same survey to fire to your contacts on a recurring cadence. Each wave tags its responses for Pre/Post, Test-retest, and trend analytics.

Schedule a pulse

Each wave will fire to every active contact. Responses are tagged with the wave label so analytics can compare them.

{n} is the wave number; {date} inserts the fire date (YYYY-MM-DD).
Respondent anonymity

Responses are anonymous. You can see how many invitations were sent and how many came back, but never which specific contact submitted which response. Reminders are routed by the system and the list of who they went to is not shown to you. If your study requires database-level anonymity, disable reminders (set Max reminders to 0).

Responses

Webhooks

Send a POST to any HTTPS URL when something happens in your workspace. Use a Slack incoming webhook URL for nicely formatted messages in a channel, a Zapier catch-hook URL to trigger any of thousands of downstream apps, or your own endpoint to wire ReliCheck into a custom system. Each request is signed with HMAC-SHA256 so you can verify it came from us.

Validated scales

Construct-aligned starter surveys with citations, target reliability, and recommended sample size. Items are paraphrased; replace with the validated wording from the cited source before publishing.

Loading…

Upload dataset

CSV or Excel (.xlsx). Up to 50,000 rows and 200 columns.

Step 1 — Pick a file

Drop a CSV or Excel file here, or browse to upload.
First row is treated as column headers. Supports .csv, .tsv, .xlsx, .xls.

Step 2 — Map your columns

Set the Likert scale your items use, then assign a type to each column. Columns marked Ignore are stored but not analyzed.
Let AI map your columns
Sends column names and a small data sample to AI. No respondent identifiers are required.
Preview (first 5 rows) and column mapping:

Add webhook

Send a JSON POST to a URL when something happens in your workspace.

Fire on

✨ Generate a survey with AI

Describe what you want to measure. The AI will draft a clean, defensible survey you can edit before you publish.

Draft preview

✨ Improve this item

Pick a rewrite to replace your question. The original stays at the top so you can compare.

Original
Suggestions
Thinking…

🌐 Translate this survey

Creates a new survey row with every prompt, scale anchor, and option translated. Your original survey stays as it is.

🎓 Methodology help

Ask any survey-methodology question. The AI knows the basics of your current survey, so it can give grounded answers.

✨ Pre-publish review

A quick AI read of your survey before respondents see it. You can apply the suggested fixes one by one, or publish anyway.

Reading your questions and predicting reliability…

🎯 Survey purpose

One or two sentences on what decision this survey is meant to inform. The clearer it is, the better the alignment check works.

Stored as part of this survey's settings. Editable any time.

🎯 Purpose alignment check

AI audits your items against the stated purpose, flags gaps, and proposes additions you can add with one click.

Reading your purpose and auditing the draft…

✨ Suggest constructs

AI groups your items into the constructs they appear to measure. Only items with an empty construct field are eligible; items you've already tagged are preserved.

Reading your items and proposing groupings…
